name: BTC Canada
title: BTC Canada — Best Crypto Exchanges for Canadians
metaDescription: Compare the best cryptocurrency exchanges for Canadians in 2026. CAD-friendly platforms with Interac e-Transfer, regulated by CSA and provincial authorities.
metaKeywords: buy bitcoin Canada, best crypto exchange Canada, Canadian crypto exchange, buy crypto with CAD
headline: Buy bitcoin in Canada
subheadline: Canadian-regulated crypto exchanges compared — Interac e-Transfer, CAD trading pairs, and CSA-compliant platforms.
introCopy: Canada has a mature crypto market with strong regulatory oversight from the CSA and provincial securities commissions. Several homegrown exchanges offer Interac e-Transfer deposits, CAD trading pairs, and compliance with Canadian tax reporting requirements.
guideContent:
Regulation

Canadian crypto exchanges must register with provincial securities regulators and FINTRAC (anti-money laundering). Look for platforms registered as Restricted Dealers with the CSA. This ensures your funds are protected under Canadian law.

Funding your account

Interac e-Transfer is the most popular and fastest deposit method — funds arrive in minutes and are usually free. Wire transfers and bank drafts are available for larger amounts. Credit card purchases carry extra fees.

Tax obligations

The CRA treats crypto as a commodity. Capital gains are 50% taxable. If you trade frequently, the CRA may classify your activity as business income (100% taxable). Keep detailed records of every transaction.
disclaimer: BTCCanada.io provides independent comparisons for Canadian crypto users. Cryptocurrency trading involves risk. This is not financial advice.
affiliateDisclosure: Some links are affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.

items.name: Shakepay
items.description: Shakepay is a Montreal-based app focused on making bitcoin and ethereum accessible to everyday Canadians. Free Interac e-Transfer deposits, a Visa card with bitcoin cashback, and a shake-to-earn bitcoin feature.
features: Shake your phone to earn bitcoin

items.name: Newton
items.description: Newton offers the tightest spreads of any Canadian exchange and no commission fees. Supports 70+ cryptocurrencies with free Interac e-Transfer deposits and withdrawals. The best option for cost-conscious Canadian traders.

items.name: Bitbuy
items.description: Bitbuy (now part of WonderFi) is one of the first Canadian exchanges to be registered with IIROC. Known for institutional-grade security, proof of reserves, and a Pro trading platform with competitive fees.

items.name: Wealthsimple Crypto
items.description: If you already use Wealthsimple for investing, adding crypto is seamless. Trade 50+ tokens alongside your TFSA and RRSP. No transfer fees, and the familiar Wealthsimple interface.

items.name: Kraken
items.description: Kraken is a veteran global exchange with full CAD support. Offers advanced trading features, staking, and some of the lowest fees available. A strong choice for experienced Canadian traders.

faqs.a: Yes. Cryptocurrency is legal in Canada and regulated by the CSA and provincial securities commissions. Exchanges must register with FINTRAC and comply with anti-money laundering requirements.

faqs.q: Can I hold crypto in my TFSA or RRSP?
faqs.a: Not directly, but some platforms like Wealthsimple offer crypto within registered accounts. You can also hold crypto ETFs (like Purpose Bitcoin ETF) in a TFSA or RRSP through any brokerage.

faqs.q: How is crypto taxed in Canada?
faqs.a: The CRA treats crypto as a commodity. Only 50% of capital gains are taxable, added to your income. If you trade frequently, the CRA may treat it as business income (100% taxable). Keep records of every transaction.

faqs.q: What's the best way to deposit CAD?
faqs.a: Interac e-Transfer is the fastest and cheapest option — most exchanges support it with no fees and near-instant deposits. Wire transfers work for larger amounts but may take 1-3 business days.
